task_completion.description: Navigate to the user management or administration section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NOSH ChartingSystem administration dashboard or user management page is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Select the target user 'John Doe' (username 'jdoe') for editing
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edit screen, form, or modal for the user 'John Doe' (username 'jdoe') is open and visible.

task_completion.description: Change the user's account status to Inactive
task_completion.visual_evidence: The status field (e.g., a dropdown, toggle, or 'Active' checkbox) for 'John Doe' is set to 'Inactive' or unchecked.
The agent clicks a 'Save', 'Update', or similar button, or a success message indicating the user was updated is visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the account is not deleted
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user list shows 'John Doe' or 'jdoe' is still present in the system (ideally with an 'Inactive' status), confirming the account was not deleted.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the NOSH ChartingSystem web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation and form interactions within a web browser, not terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use database commands to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible executing MySQL/MariaDB queries to update the user status.

integrity.description: The agent used the genuine application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches a real web application (NOSH ChartingSystem) and not a text editor or static mock-up.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target username is 'jdoe' with role 'assistant'. The task requires changing the user's status to inactive without deleting the account. The agent must accomplish this via the NOSH ChartingSystem web interface.
